card.intuition: A method used to monitor and profile request flows as they travel through a complex web of microservices, using a unique Trace ID passed in headers.
card.analogy: A passport with stamps: as a traveler (Request) goes through different countries (Services), each border control agent stamps the same passport with a record of when they arrived and when they left. At the end, you can see the whole journey in one book.
card.time_complexity: O(1) to tag; O(N) to aggregate
when_to_use: Debugging 'Why is this slow?' in a system with many internal service calls
Identifying which specific service in a chain is failing or throwing errors
Visualizing dependencies between services in real-time
gotchas: Storage Cost: tracing every single request can produce Terabytes of data per hour. Most systems use 'Sampling' to only trace 1% or 0.1% of requests.
Instrumentation: every service needs to be 'taught' how to pass the Trace ID. If one service in the middle forgets, the trace is broken.

exercise.prompt: Identify the term for a single unit of work within a trace, representing a start and end time in one service.
exercise.test_code:
def check(ans): return ans.lower() == "span"
assert check("Span")
print("PASS")
